h11h_audit.exact_boundary: H11-H is a narrow combined POVC artifact, not a broad training or benchmark claim

nonclaims: updateable QNN context for Gemma training
new corpus scale beyond the 16-sequence HF-derived Phase 12 cache
promoted_claims: H11-H artifact hygiene passed and Phase 12 reports keep raw tensor/checkpoint payloads out of git-facing report directories.

A compact-artifact strategy is implemented for long runs: reports pull JSON telemetry/manifests/checksum chains only, while raw f32/u32/u8/qnn/dlc payloads stay on phone, RunPod, or temporary directories.
The current trainable scope supports rank-16 and rank-32 post-layer0 residual adapters with AdamW, weight decay, gradient clipping, optimizer-state checkpointing, and phone-local queue execution.
Phone-native C++ tokenizer/cache path created or reused a 16-sequence HF-derived corpus cache on the REDMAGIC SM8750 with no host minibatch serving during training.
Rank-16 residual top-k KL training improved heldout KL from 1.1291671353 fixed control to 1.0005755997 after 8 iterations, with mini metric 0.1137876804 to 0.1233203377.

A disconnect-safe phone-local sequential continuation selected LR 3e-4: heldout KL improved from the 8-step reference 1.0005755997 to 0.8207082971, mini metric from 0.1233203377 to 0.1625584151, and agreement from 0.1140456182 to 0.2244897959.
Single high-gradient adapter_a and adapter_b finite-difference probes matched phone-produced top-k KL gradients with relative errors below 2e-6 for the residual adapter path.

QAIRT host tooling was repaired enough to run converter/importer/updater help and generate attempted updateable DLC/context artifacts, but current updateable context generation was falsified before phone applyBinarySection proof.

A measured HTP frozen-forward context run exists, but the integrated heterogeneous Gemma learning path was falsified because the available HTP context is incompatible with Gemma hidden size and training island.
